title: Game Functions — Modular Design
description: Refactor your game into clean, reusable functions. Create these five functions: move_player(player_state, rooms, destination) that returns a message string, look_around(player_state, rooms) that returns the room description, take_item(player_state, room_items, item_name) that moves an item from room_items to inventory, show_inventory(player_state) that returns a formatted inventory string, and use_item(player_state, item_name) that uses and removes an item (potions heal, returns a message).
starter_code:
# Text Adventure — Step 6: Game Functions
# Extract game logic into clean, reusable functions.

rooms = {
    'entrance': 'You stand at the entrance of a dark cave.',
    'hallway': 'A long hallway with torches on the walls.',
    'treasure_room': 'A glittering room full of gold and jewels.',
}

game_items = [
    {'name': 'iron_sword', 'type': 'weapon', 'value': 10},
    {'name': 'health_potion', 'type': 'potion', 'value': 25},
    {'name': 'rusty_key', 'type': 'key', 'value': 'treasure_room'},
    {'name': 'wooden_shield', 'type': 'weapon', 'value': 5},
]

# Items present in each room (room_name -> list of item dicts)
room_items = {
    'entrance': [game_items[0]],
    'hallway': [game_items[1], game_items[3]],
    'treasure_room': [game_items[2]],
}

player_state = {
    'name': 'Hero',
    'health': 100,
    'max_health': 100,
    'room': 'entrance',
    'inventory': [],
}

def move_player(player_state, rooms, destination):
    """Move player to destination room. Return a message string."""
    # If destination exists in rooms, update player_state['room'] and return description
    # If not, return 'There is no room called <destination>'
    pass

def look_around(player_state, rooms):
    """Return the description of the player's current room."""
    pass

def take_item(player_state, room_items, item_name):
    """Take an item from the current room and add to inventory.
    Returns a message string."""
    # Find the item in room_items[player_state['room']]
    # Move it to player_state['inventory']
    # Return 'Picked up <item_name>' or 'No item called <item_name> here'
    pass

def show_inventory(player_state):
    """Return a formatted string of inventory contents."""
    # Return each item name, or 'Inventory is empty'
    pass

def use_item(player_state, item_name):
    """Use an item from inventory. Potions heal the player.
    Returns a message string."""
    # Find item in inventory, apply effect, remove it
    # Potions: heal by item['value'], cap at max_health
    # Other items: 'You can\'t use that'
    # Not found: 'You don\'t have <item_name>'
    pass

# Test
print(look_around(player_state, rooms))
print(move_player(player_state, rooms, 'hallway'))
print(take_item(player_state, room_items, 'health_potion'))
print(show_inventory(player_state))
player_state['health'] = 60
print(use_item(player_state, 'health_potion'))
print(f"Health after potion: {player_state['health']}")
test_code:
assert callable(move_player), 'move_player should be a function'
assert callable(look_around), 'look_around should be a function'
assert callable(take_item), 'take_item should be a function'
assert callable(show_inventory), 'show_inventory should be a function'
assert callable(use_item), 'use_item should be a function'
test_rooms = {'start': 'Start room.', 'end': 'End room.'}
test_state = {'name': 'T', 'health': 50, 'max_health': 100, 'room': 'start', 'inventory': []}
result = look_around(test_state, test_rooms)
assert 'Start room' in result, f"look_around should return room description, got: {result}"
move_result = move_player(test_state, test_rooms, 'end')
assert test_state['room'] == 'end', 'move_player should update room'
bad_move = move_player(test_state, test_rooms, 'nowhere')
assert test_state['room'] == 'end', 'Invalid move should not change room'
assert 'nowhere' in bad_move.lower(), f"Bad move message should mention destination, got: {bad_move}"
test_room_items = {'end': [{'name': 'gem', 'type': 'key', 'value': 'door'}]}
take_result = take_item(test_state, test_room_items, 'gem')
assert any(i['name'] == 'gem' for i in test_state['inventory']), 'Item should be in inventory after take'
assert len(test_room_items['end']) == 0, 'Item should be removed from room'
take_missing = take_item(test_state, test_room_items, 'nothing')
assert 'nothing' in take_missing.lower(), f"Missing item message should mention item name, got: {take_missing}"
inv_str = show_inventory(test_state)
assert 'gem' in inv_str.lower(), f"show_inventory should list items, got: {inv_str}"
test_state['inventory'] = [{'name': 'potion', 'type': 'potion', 'value': 30}]
test_state['health'] = 50
use_result = use_item(test_state, 'potion')
assert test_state['health'] == 80, f"Health should be 80 after 30hp potion from 50, got {test_state['health']}"
assert len(test_state['inventory']) == 0, 'Potion should be removed after use'
missing_use = use_item(test_state, 'nothing')
assert 'nothing' in missing_use.lower(), f"Missing item use message should mention name, got: {missing_use}"
acceptance_criteria: move_player() moves to valid rooms, rejects invalid ones
take_item() moves item from room to inventory
show_inventory() lists items or says empty
use_item() applies potion effects and removes the item
hints: move_player: check 'if destination in rooms' before updating
take_item: loop through room_items.get(player_state['room'], []) to find the item
use_item: find the item in inventory, check type == 'potion', then heal
Use min(health + value, max_health) to cap healing
Remember to remove items from their source list after taking/using them
